question: Define an abstract base class `Printable` with a pure virtual method `print()`. Derive two concrete classes, `Document` and `Image`, from `Printable`. Implement the `print()` method in each derived class to display appropriate information (e.g., 'Printing Document: [title]', 'Printing Image: [filename]').

test_cases.input: Polymorphic print and delete of Document ('Memo_to_staff')
Polymorphic print and delete of Image ('logo.svg')

errors: Poorly chosen and non-descriptive variable names (e.g., `doc_title`, `img_file`, `d_obj`, `i_obj`, `s_s`, `old_buf`).
Constructors for `Document` and `Image` take `std::string` by value instead of `const std::string&`, leading to unnecessary string copies.
Inconsistent use of `std::endl` and `\n` for newlines; `std::endl` forces a flush, which can be less efficient if not explicitly required.
Inconsistent indentation and formatting, particularly in class definitions and method implementations.
